trajectory_summary: The call builds from neutral to technically engaged and ends with a booked in-person appointment — a positive outcome. However, the booking is fragile: the wife (who filled out the form and is on the title) is not confirmed on the appointment, the full button-up protocol was skipped, and Bucky's legitimacy positioning was entirely passive throughout Andy's three-touch vetting sequence. The energy built organically from Andy's personality, not from Bucky's deliberate moves.
outcome_dispute: The call ends with a booked appointment, so the outcome tag should be 'appointment_booked.' However, the appointment is structurally fragile: the initiating decision-maker (wife, on title) is not confirmed present, and the button-up protocol was not completed. This is a conditional booking, not a clean sit.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Spouse-protocol discipline — confirmed title co-holder not included in appointment
primary_coaching_focus.why: The wife filled out the form, is on the title, and is the initiating decision-maker. Bucky confirmed her existence twice and never asked whether she would be on the appointment. The booking was made with Andy alone. Per protocols.spouse_decision, this is the single highest-risk gap in the call — the appointment is structurally vulnerable because the person who initiated the inquiry is unconfirmed as present. This is a mechanical protocol failure, not a judgment call, and it directly threatens the sit.

key_moments.quote: I didn't fill out any solar form, but I wouldn't be surprised if my wife did.
key_moments.stewart_read: Andy's opening line tells Bucky two things simultaneously: (1) the wife is the initiating decision-maker, and (2) Andy is not personally invested in the call yet. Bucky acknowledged and moved on without asking whether the wife should be part of the appointment. This is the first of two missed spouse-inclusion opportunities.

Second touch of the spouse pattern. Bucky confirms the wife is on title and moves directly to utility company. At this point the protocol is unambiguous: both title-holders should be confirmed on the appointment. Bucky did not ask. The appointment was booked with Andy alone — the person who did NOT fill out the form.

key_moments.quote: That's something that I don't pay any attention to. I'm a retired soldier. Oh. A physicist, electrical engineer electrical engineer and a mathematician with three PhDs. So I pay attention to math and science, not the money.
key_moments.stewart_read: Andy's credential disclosure is not a deflection — it's a trust gift and a qualification signal. A retired military physicist with three PhDs who 'pays attention to math and science' is telling Bucky exactly how to frame the closer's appointment: lead with the engineering logic of the system design, not the savings pitch. Bucky responded with a filler pause and moved to the next verify item.

key_moments.quote: I'm running your text through a, an AI system to find out whether you're real or bullshit.
key_moments.stewart_read: Andy is running an active legitimacy check mid-call. This is the third touch of the vetting pattern. Bucky waited passively and was cleared. The strategic read would be that Bucky had already established enough legitimacy to survive the check — but the evidence doesn't support deliberate setup. Bucky never offered Ion's Tesla installer status, website, or physical address at any point. He passed by luck of Andy's AI tool, not by proactive legitimacy anchoring.
